description: Layered quality platform: 7 curated themes, analyst writing playbook in skill.md, expanded rule-based coach (40+ rules), and blocking LLM review gate with actionable fix instructions.

userStories.title: Expand Theme dataclass and define Corporate + Dark themes
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the Theme dataclass expanded with new tokens so all 7 themes can express chart sizing, numeric typography, density, and responsive behavior.
acceptanceCriteria: Theme dataclass gains 7 new fields: chart_default_width (str, default '100%'), chart_viewbox_width (int, default 760), number_font (str), density (str: compact|standard|spacious), heading_scale (float), kpi_card_min_width (str), table_number_align (str: left|right)
All new fields have defaults so existing Theme instantiations still work
Corporate theme defined: Inter font, electric blue accent, density='standard', heading_scale=1.333, number_font=Inter, table_number_align='left'
Dark theme updated with new tokens: density='standard', heading_scale=1.333, number_font=Inter
Existing 'default' key in THEMES dict maps to Corporate theme
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Define Executive, Financial, and Consulting themes
userStories.description: As a developer, I need three business-context themes so reports can match their audience.
acceptanceCriteria: Executive theme: density='spacious', heading_scale=1.5, generous whitespace, number_font=Inter, unique 8-color chart palette
Financial theme: density='compact', heading_scale=1.25, number_font='Tabular Mono, ui-monospace, monospace', table_number_align='right', muted blue-gray palette
Consulting theme: density='standard', heading_scale=1.333, navy+teal chart palette, number_font=Inter
All three added to THEMES dict
get_theme('executive'), get_theme('financial'), get_theme('consulting') return correct themes
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Define Technical and Editorial themes, update layout widths
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the remaining two themes and adjusted layout widths.
acceptanceCriteria: Technical theme: density='compact', heading_scale=1.25, number_font='JetBrains Mono, ui-monospace, monospace', table_number_align='right', neutral gray palette
Editorial theme: density='spacious', heading_scale=1.333, font_stack uses Georgia/Charter serif, wider line_height, number_font=Georgia
Both added to THEMES dict
THEMES dict has 7 entries total: default, dark, executive, financial, consulting, technical, editorial
Narrow layout width updated from 720px to 680px in LAYOUT_WIDTHS
get_theme() falls back to Corporate (default) for unknown names
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Enforce full-width chart containers in renderers
userStories.description: As a developer, I need all chart containers to render at 100% width so charts fill available space on all devices.
acceptanceCriteria: All chart section renderers (_render_bar_chart, _render_line_chart, etc.) wrap SVG in div with style='width:100%; display:block;'
SVG elements use width='100%' and viewBox using theme.chart_viewbox_width
Markdown chart blocks (```chart```) also get full-width containers
data-or-chart attribute preserved on chart containers
Existing chart rendering still produces valid HTML
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Add responsive container wrapper with media queries
userStories.description: As a developer, I need _wrap_container() to include responsive CSS so reports reflow correctly on tablet and mobile.
acceptanceCriteria: _wrap_container() generates a unique container ID and includes a scoped <style> block
columns layout stacks vertically on mobile (<600px), side-by-side on desktop
KPI grid flex-wrap: 3-4 per row desktop, 2 tablet, 1 mobile (via min-width on flex children)
Tables wrapped with overflow-x:auto for horizontal scroll
Container padding: 48px desktop, 32px tablet (600-960px), 16px mobile (<600px)
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Apply new theme tokens in KPI, table, and heading renderers
userStories.description: As a developer, I need section renderers to use the new theme tokens for typography and density.
acceptanceCriteria: KPI grid renderer uses theme.number_font for metric value font-family
KPI grid renderer uses theme.kpi_card_min_width for flex item min-width
Table renderer applies theme.table_number_align to numeric-looking cells
Heading renderers (h1-h4) calculate font-size from theme.heading_scale ratio
Spacing (padding, margins) on sections adapts based on theme.density: compact=tighter, standard=current, spacious=more room
Slideshow content_type increases effective heading_scale by 0.1
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Add Voice & Tone section to skill.md
userStories.description: As an agent, I need voice and tone rules in skill.md so I write in analyst briefing style on the first attempt.
acceptanceCriteria: Voice & Tone section added after content formats table in SKILL.md
Includes 3+ 'Do' examples of analyst briefing style (third-person, factual)
Includes 4+ 'Don't' examples of AI filler language
5 numbered rules: no first person, no superlatives, no hedging in structured fields, every paragraph earns its space, lead with the headline
SKILL.md YAML frontmatter version bumped
Existing content preserved

userStories.title: Add Analytics Playbook to skill.md
userStories.description: As an agent, I need a chart selection decision tree and KPI framing rules so I pick the right visualization and contextualize every metric.
acceptanceCriteria: Analytics Playbook section added after chart types section
Chart selection table maps 7 data shapes to chart types with reasoning
Anti-rules: no pie >6 segments, no bar for time series, no line for categorical
KPI framing table with bad/good examples for value, delta, trend fields
Data integrity rules: no extrapolation, state missing data, units in dataset names
Existing content preserved

userStories.title: Add Design Guidance and update Themes section in skill.md
userStories.description: As an agent, I need layout selection guidance, section ordering rules, and documentation of all 7 themes.
acceptanceCriteria: Report Design section added after layouts section
Layout selection table maps 5 content types to recommended layouts
Chart sizing rule stated: all charts fill available width, no fixed pixels
Section ordering guide: summary-header -> kpi-grid -> charts/tables -> text -> callout -> action-items
Themes table updated to document all 7 themes with personality and best-for guidance
Updated SKILL.md is between 650-700 lines total
Existing content preserved

userStories.title: Add AI filler detection rules to authoring coach
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the coach to detect and block AI-generated filler language in summaries and body text.
acceptanceCriteria: AI_FILLER_PHRASES constant list with >=30 known AI filler patterns defined at module level
ai_filler_summary rule: error severity when summary contains any filler phrase (case-insensitive match)
ai_filler_body rule: warning severity when body plain text contains >=3 distinct filler phrases
summary_is_meta rule: warning when summary starts with 'This report covers', 'In this report', 'The following report', 'Below you will find'
No false positives on the phrase 'it is worth $2.4M' (legitimate business language)
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Add writing style rules to authoring coach
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the coach to flag superlatives in structured fields, first-person voice, and verbose paragraphs.
acceptanceCriteria: superlative_in_structured: warning when KPI labels, chart headings, or summary contain 'incredible', 'amazing', 'outstanding', 'impressive', 'groundbreaking' (not in quoted text)
first_person_voice: warning when body text uses 'I ', 'we ', 'our ' more than twice
paragraph_too_verbose: warning when any paragraph >600 chars and <1 number per 200 chars
paragraph_density threshold updated from 1200 to 800 chars
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Add analytics rules to authoring coach
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the coach to flag KPIs without context and mismatched chart types.
acceptanceCriteria: kpi_missing_delta: warning when KPI metric has value but no delta or trend
kpi_delta_is_label: warning when delta contains only a name with no number, %, or comparison operator
chart_type_mismatch_time: warning when chart labels match time-series patterns (month names, Q1-Q4, W1-W52, date-like) but chart type is bar-chart
chart_type_mismatch_ranking: info when >5 categories with single dataset uses vertical bar-chart instead of horizontal-bar-chart
pie_too_many_segments: warning when pie/donut chart has >6 segments
chart_no_heading: warning when any chart section has no heading field
evaluate_authoring_quality() accepts structured section data for KPI and chart analysis
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Add design rules to authoring coach
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the coach to flag poor structural choices like missing owners on action items and skipped heading levels.
acceptanceCriteria: chart_fixed_width: warning when SVG container lacks width:100% in style
chart_fixed_dimensions: severity escalated from info to warning
chart_hardcoded_colors: threshold reduced from >5 to >4 unique colors
no_summary_header: info when structured body has no summary-header as first section
action_items_no_owner: warning when action items lack owner field
action_items_no_due: warning when action items lack due field
table_single_column: warning when table has only 1 column
consecutive_text_sections: info when 3+ text sections in a row with no visual break
heading_depth_skip: warning when heading levels skip (h1->h3 without h2)
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Add slide rules and recalibrate coach scoring
userStories.description: As a developer, I need slide-specific quality rules and rebalanced scoring weights for the expanded rule set.
acceptanceCriteria: slide_too_dense: warning when slide has >2 sections (only when content_type='slideshow')
slide_text_heavy: warning when slide text section >300 chars
slide_no_takeaway: info when chart slide has no accompanying callout
slide_bullet_count: warning when slide bullet list has >4 items
Scoring weights updated: error=-20, warning=-6, info=-2 (from -25/-10/-4)
Total coach rules: 40+
Coach evaluation completes in <100ms on a sample report
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Create LLM review module with prompt and rubric
userStories.description: As a developer, I need an LLM review module that evaluates reports across writing, analytics, and design dimensions using Claude Haiku.
acceptanceCriteria: New file: backend/app/core/llm_review.py
Review function accepts rendered HTML, report metadata (title, summary, tags, theme, content_type), and coach results
Review prompt includes rubric definitions for 3 dimensions (writing, analytics, design) scored 1-5
Prompt instructs LLM to output structured JSON with scores, issues, and specific fix instructions
Slide-specific rubric adjustments when content_type='slideshow'
Response parsed into a ReviewResult dataclass with scores, issues, fix_instructions, status
Blocking logic: min 3 per dimension, min 3.5 average
Uses anthropic SDK to call Claude Haiku (model from LLM_REVIEW_MODEL env var)
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Add timeout, caching, and configuration to LLM review
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the LLM review to handle failures gracefully, cache results, and be fully configurable via env vars.
acceptanceCriteria: Timeout: configurable via LLM_REVIEW_TIMEOUT_MS (default 15000). On timeout, returns status='skipped'
API errors: caught and return status='skipped' (never blocks on infra failure)
Cache: content-hash keyed, 5-minute TTL. Identical resubmissions skip LLM call if previous review passed

Configuration env vars: LLM_REVIEW_ENABLED (default true), LLM_REVIEW_MODE (enforce|shadow), LLM_REVIEW_MIN_SCORE (default 3), LLM_REVIEW_MIN_AVG (default 3.5), LLM_REVIEW_MODEL, LLM_REVIEW_TIMEOUT_MS
get_llm_review_mode() helper function similar to get_authoring_coach_mode()
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Integrate LLM review into publish pipeline
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the LLM review wired into the report publish endpoint so it runs after the rule-based coach.
acceptanceCriteria: LLM review called in routes/reports.py publish endpoint after rule-based coach passes
LLM review skipped when rule-based coach status is 'blocked'
When LLM_REVIEW_MODE='enforce' and review blocks: return 422 with llm_review field containing scores, issues, fix_instructions
When LLM_REVIEW_MODE='shadow': review runs and logs but does not block publish
When LLM_REVIEW_ENABLED=false: review skipped entirely
Successful publish response includes quality object: coach_score, coach_status, llm_review (scores + status)
Existing 422 response shape for coach blocks unchanged
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Update SDK with ReviewBlockedError and quality response
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the Python SDK to handle LLM review 422 responses and expose quality scores.
acceptanceCriteria: New ReviewBlockedError exception in sdk/openreporting/exceptions.py with scores, issues, fix_instructions attributes
client.publish() raises ReviewBlockedError on LLM review 422 (distinct from CoachBlockedError)
ReportResponse model gains optional quality field (coach_score, coach_status, llm_review)
dry_run() unchanged (runs coach only, no LLM review)
preview() unchanged
Old SDK versions treat new 422 shapes as generic errors (no breaking changes)
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Add brand override accessibility validation
userStories.description: As a developer, I need brand color overrides validated for WCAG contrast and chart color distinguishability.
acceptanceCriteria: New function validate_brand_overrides(theme) returns list of advisory warnings
Checks text_color vs bg_color contrast ratio meets WCAG AA (4.5:1 for body text)
Checks heading_color vs bg_color contrast ratio meets WCAG AA (3:1 for large text)
Checks chart_colors palette: minimum deltaE of 20 between adjacent colors
Results are advisory warnings only, never blocking
apply_brand_overrides() calls validation and attaches warnings to result
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
